description:
## Background

Switching is the must-have multi-workspace behavior, and it is where a process-global cwd or eager UI update would be most harmful. A user must never see Issue Explorer data from Workspace A under Workspace B or lose a good snapshot because a candidate fails. Concurrent selections also need deterministic behavior.

## What to build

Make selecting a known Workspace or choosing another folder an atomic switch. While a candidate validates and loads, retain the old Current Workspace and complete Issue Explorer snapshot. Commit and display the target only after all views and the current/MRU store update succeed. Provide retryable, dismissible feedback for post-validation failures.

## Technical Approach

- Assign every switch request a monotonically increasing generation. A newer selection immediately supersedes an earlier Pending Workspace; stale results must be discarded after each asynchronous boundary and must never persist or publish.
- Keep the target Pending, never Current, through validation and All/Ready/Blocked loading. The selector may identify the pending target, but the visible Current Workspace and Issue Explorer snapshot remain the old ones.
- Validation failures use inline selector feedback. After validation succeeds, retain the target in the catalog even if later loading fails. Load or persistence failure with an old snapshot uses a dismissible banner with retry; it must preserve the old state.
- Treat the final store save of `currentWorkspacePath` and MRU promotion as the commit boundary. If it fails, retain the old Current Workspace and do not publish target data.
- The backend is the final stale-result guard; frontend generation checking is an additional defense for events or responses that arrive out of order.

## Considerations

- Initial startup has no old snapshot; its failed restore remains an empty chooser case owned by the catalog slice.
- Do not add automatic background refresh in this work. Switching performs an immediate full load only.
- Retry must run the same transaction and still respect a later user selection.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Switching from Workspace A to Workspace B leaves A visibly Current until B validates, all Issue Explorer views load, and the store commit succeeds.
- [ ] A validation, loading, or persistence failure keeps A Current and preserves its Issue Explorer snapshot; relevant failures have the specified inline or dismissible retry feedback.
- [ ] A valid target that fails loading remains a retryable known Workspace without MRU promotion.
- [ ] When selections race, only the latest generation can publish or persist; a stale earlier result is silent.
- [ ] Successful switching updates both Current Workspace and MRU order exactly once.

## Test Plan

- Rust state-machine tests for success, validation failure, load failure, persistence failure, retry, and stale-generation outcomes.
- TauRPC/frontend integration tests proving old-snapshot preservation, pending state, banners, retry, and stale-event rejection.
- Real desktop WebDriver coverage with two distinguishable disposable Beadwork repositories: select A, switch to B, and attempt an invalid target while B is Current.
- Run `cargo test`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm check`, TypeScript checking, and the workspace-management desktop suite.

## Transaction and interaction amendments

A Pending Workspace is cancellable. Cancel must invalidate its request generation, clear pending presentation, retain the old Current Workspace snapshot when one exists, and return an initial user with no snapshot to the empty chooser. A newer selection has the same invalidation effect and should take precedence immediately. Where the command layer can stop obsolete read-only child processes safely, it should; correctness never depends on killing them because stale results are rejected by generation.

The visible Workspace transition includes Issue Explorer interaction context. On successful commit, clear the selected Issue and local Issue Search before the target snapshot is interactive; Issue IDs can overlap across repositories and a search query is scoped to its prior Workspace. Preserve the selected Issue List View if it remains a valid lens. Dismissing an error banner hides only session feedback and does not remove the known retryable target.

All pending, retry, cancellation, removal, and final commit operations use the serialized backend state service. Removing a Pending target invalidates it; removing Current while another target is Pending clears Current and invalidates the pending request rather than allowing an automatic replacement.

### Additional acceptance criteria

- [ ] A user can cancel a Pending switch without losing the old Current Workspace snapshot.
- [ ] A successful switch cannot retain selected Issue Detail or Issue Search state from the prior Workspace.
- [ ] A stale, cancelled, or removed target never publishes or persists after its generation is invalidated.
- [ ] Dismissing a failure banner preserves the retryable catalog entry and does not alter Current Workspace.

### Additional test plan

- Test cancel with and without a prior Current Workspace, then deliver the cancelled command result and prove it is ignored.
- Test removal of Pending and Current Workspaces while work is in flight.
- Test overlapping Issue IDs and active search text across two Workspaces to prove detail and search reset.
- Test banner dismissal separately from retry and catalog removal.

comments.text:
## Atomicity rule

The visible Current Workspace and Issue Explorer snapshot are one unit. No target title, path, or issue data may become current until the entire candidate transaction and its durability commit succeed. Generation checks protect this rule when user selections overlap.

Changelog: atomic switching now has additional invariants. A successful switch must clear the selected Issue and local Issue Search before the new snapshot is interactive, while preserving the selected Issue List View when it remains meaningful. The selector must expose Cancel while Pending; cancel increments/invalidate generation, retains the old snapshot, and prevents a late result from publishing. Dismissing a failure banner hides only that session feedback, not the retryable catalog entry. Removal of a Pending or Current Workspace must invalidate pending work through the state service.

Changelog: expanded the task body with cancellation, selection/search reset, banner-dismissal semantics, removal races, and explicit acceptance/tests. These are product-safety requirements, not optional polish.

# Implementation Plan

## Goal

Make workspace switching a cancellable, asynchronous, generation-guarded transaction that preserves the prior Current Workspace snapshot until a fully loaded and durable target commit succeeds.

## Tasks

1. **Refactor the workspace service into cancellable transaction phases without weakening its commit boundary.**
   - File: `src-tauri/src/workspace.rs`
   - Changes: Keep `begin_selection` as the only creator of a monotonically increasing request generation and Pending Workspace, but expose phase-level operations usable without holding the runtime mutex during subprocess work: validate the request candidate, durably retain the validated root in the catalog, load the three views, and durably publish the loaded result. Each mutation must check the request generation before and after a store save; failure handling must only clear Pending/set error for the still-current request.
   - Changes: Add `cancel_pending` to increment/invalidate generation, clear Pending and transient error, retain Current/catalog/snapshot, and perform no store write. Preserve the existing `remove_workspace`, reset, and newer-selection invalidation behavior; removal must continue to invalidate a pending request before a late worker can publish.
   - Changes: Keep the synchronous composition method needed by startup restoration/unit tests, but make it delegate to the same phase methods so manual switches and restoration retain one state-machine contract.
   - Acceptance: A stale, cancelled, or removed request cannot run a later mutation, save a catalog/current state, publish a target, or replace the prior Current Workspace. Successful switching has exactly the validated-catalog save followed by one final Current+MRU save.

2. **Resolve and model the retry-target contract for post-validation failures before implementing banner actions.**
   - File: `src-tauri/src/workspace.rs`
   - File: `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs`
   - Changes: Decide how a load/final-save failure identifies the already-validated candidate for Retry after `pendingWorkspace` is cleared. Add an explicit ephemeral retry target to `WorkspaceState`, or an equivalent typed error context, and clear it on new selection, cancel, removal, reset, and successful commit. Do not reuse `pendingWorkspace`, because its established definition is “currently loading.”
   - Acceptance: A `loadFailed` or switch-specific `storeSaveFailed` response identifies exactly the candidate that Retry must submit; that candidate remains in the catalog without MRU promotion. Banner dismissal does not erase it.

3. **Make the TauRPC switch resolver asynchronous and publish ordered workspace transition updates.**
   - File: `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs`
   - Changes: Do not hold `WorkspaceRuntime`'s `Mutex` while `bw`/`git` run. Under the lock, begin the request and capture its request token; emit a Pending workspace-transition update; execute validation and the three-view load with `tokio::task::spawn_blocking`/`ProcessRunner`; reacquire the serialized service only for generation-checked retain, failure, final commit, snapshot assignment, cancel, and removal operations.
   - Changes: Emit a typed frontend transition payload after Pending, current-request failure, cancellation/removal, and successful publication. The payload must include `WorkspaceState` (and include complete `LoadIssueExplorerDataResponse` only on a committed success). Emit no failure/success transition for a stale completion. Assign `WorkspaceRuntime.snapshot` in the same critical section as the final service publication, never during Pending or failure.
   - Changes: Add `cancel_workspace() -> WorkspaceState` to `BeadsmithApi`; it invokes `cancel_pending`, leaves the prior snapshot unchanged, and emits the resulting state. Keep `switch_workspace` as the typed transport, returning its final success response for existing direct callers while the transition event provides immediate Pending/failure UI updates.
   - Changes: Regenerate `src/rpc/bindings.ts` through the existing Rust binding-generation test; extend the binding assertions for `cancel_workspace` and any newly exported transition/retry type. Do not manually maintain generated content.
   - Acceptance: A second RPC can begin or cancel while an earlier worker is blocked in a Beadwork command; the backend drops the earlier result after each asynchronous boundary. The renderer can render Pending before the final switch response resolves.

4. **Apply generation-aware transition state in the application shell and reset workspace-scoped Issue Explorer interaction on commit.**
   - File: `src/App.tsx`
   - Changes: Subscribe on mount to the backend workspace-transition event and clean up its listener on unmount. Centralize application of transition events/RPC responses: reject a response/event whose generation is older than the most recently accepted generation, retain the old `issueState` for Pending and failure states, and accept issue data only when it accompanies a matching committed Current Workspace transition.
   - Changes: Change `selectWorkspace` to start the same RPC transaction without eagerly replacing the Issue Explorer snapshot. Its rejected stale result must be ignored silently; non-stale transport failures may refresh typed workspace state but must not replace issue data. Route Retry through this same handler using the resolved retry target.
   - Changes: Add a confirmed-workspace-commit key/version to the `IssueExplorer` element. Advance it only when the target becomes Current with a matching complete snapshot; React then clears `searchQuery` and `selectedIssueId`, which are local to `IssueExplorer`. Keep `activeIssueListViewId` in `App` so the selected valid Issue List View survives the remount.
   - Changes: Update cancel, removal, memory retry/reset, and initial-load paths to use the shared state application guard. Removing Current must replace the local snapshot with the empty chooser state; cancelling/removing only Pending must leave it untouched.
   - Acceptance: During A→B Pending/failure, A’s name/path/Issue Explorer data remain rendered. On confirmed B commit, no prior selected detail or search query can appear with B, while the selected Issue List View remains selected. Late A events/responses cannot overwrite B.

5. **Add Cancel, failure-banner, Retry, and Dismiss presentation to the workspace selector.**
   - File: `src/components/WorkspaceSelector.tsx`
   - Changes: Extend props for `onCancel`, switch retry, and local banner dismissal. Show the pending candidate identity plus a Cancel control whenever `pendingWorkspace` exists, including the no-Current empty-chooser case; disabling the old snapshot is not allowed.
   - Changes: Keep validation and Git-root errors as inline selector feedback. Preserve the dedicated unreadable-store recovery panel for `storeReadFailed`. Render a dismissible banner (not the full storage recovery panel) for a post-validation `loadFailed` or switch-finalization `storeSaveFailed` when there is a retry target; Retry invokes the normal switch handler with that target and Dismiss only hides session UI.
   - Changes: Store dismissal visibility locally keyed to the error/request identity so a state refresh does not remove catalog data, a new selection/new error can display again, and no dismiss operation calls remove/reset/mutates backend error state.
   - Acceptance: Cancel calls only the cancel RPC; Retry replays the candidate transaction; Dismiss leaves Current Workspace and the retryable catalog entry intact. Inline validation feedback is not incorrectly rendered as a banner.

6. **Expand Rust state-machine and RPC contract tests for cancellation, phase races, and generated bindings.**
   - File: `src-tauri/src/workspace.rs`
   - File: `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs`
   - Changes: Add deterministic fake-runner/state-machine tests for: cancel with and without an existing Current Workspace; cancellation after validation and during load; a late cancelled completion; removal of Pending and removal of Current while another request is in flight; a newer selection overtaking an older request; retry after a retained load failure; final-store failure; and exactly-once Current/MRU promotion. Assert store writes and command invocations at each boundary, not only final state.
   - Changes: Test the RPC orchestration with a controllably blocked command/worker or an extracted orchestration seam: Pending transition is published before blocking work completes, cancel/newer selection can acquire state while it runs, stale worker completion produces no transition/snapshot mutation, and the bindings contain the new RPC/types.
   - Acceptance: `cargo test` proves every amended issue invariant, including no post-cancel persistence/publication and path-free issue loading.

7. **Add frontend integration tests for state preservation, interaction reset, and selector controls.**
   - File: `src/App.test.tsx`
   - File: `src/components/WorkspaceSelector.test.tsx`
   - Changes: Mock the workspace transition subscription and deferred switch promises. Cover A remaining visible during B Pending, A surviving validation/load/store-save failures, Cancel with and without Current, retry dispatching the failure candidate, same-generation completion versus late older-generation response/event, and Pending/Current removal races.
   - Changes: In `App.test.tsx`, select an Issue and enter search text in A, switch to B containing the same Issue ID, and assert the B detail pane is empty and `#issue-search` is empty while the active list-view control remains selected.
   - Changes: In `WorkspaceSelector.test.tsx`, cover pending candidate/Cancel, inline `validationFailed` and `gitRootFailed`, load/save banner Retry/Dismiss behavior, `storeReadFailed` recovery isolation, and dismissal persistence across a harmless state refresh.
   - Acceptance: `pnpm test` proves frontend behavior without relying on accidental React-state retention or IPC response ordering.

8. **Extend real-desktop workspace-switch coverage with deterministic pending and distinct-workspace fixtures.**
   - File: `e2e/issue-list/fixtures/workspace.ts`
   - File: `e2e/issue-list/scripts/run-scenario.ts`
   - File: `wdio.issue-list.conf.ts`
   - File: `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.success.spec.ts` (or new `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.atomic.spec.ts`)
   - Changes: Create distinct A/B Beadwork fixtures (including deliberately overlapping Issue IDs where the reset test needs them) while retaining a separate true-empty fixture for the existing empty scenario. Add a test-only, temporary `bw` wrapper/delay control in the scenario launcher—not application production code—so a target command can be held long enough to observe Pending and invoke Cancel through the real typed `switch_workspace` transport.
   - Changes: Prove real desktop startup empty → A success; A remains rendered while B is Pending; cancel leaves A; a subsequent B commit replaces A; invalid selection while B is Current renders inline feedback and preserves B; and active search/selected detail from A reset on B. Add banner/retry coverage here only if the wrapper can deterministically fail a post-validation view load without leaking platform-specific behavior into production code.
   - Acceptance: `pnpm test:e2e:issue-list` runs against the built binary, never changes process cwd/writes the store directly, and confirms the composed backend/RPC/event/renderer transaction.

9. **Reconcile developer-facing WebDriver documentation and record the plan on the issue.**
   - File: `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md`
   - Changes: Replace the obsolete statement that Pending/concurrency presentation requires future `bsm-kia.4` asynchronous work with the final supported scenario, typed transport/event behavior, and any test wrapper caveat.
   - Changes: After review, post this complete plan as a comment on Beadwork issue `bsm-kia.4` using the repository’s normal `bw` workflow.
   - Acceptance: Documentation matches the implemented test surface, and the issue history contains the plan for later agents.

## Files to Modify

- `src-tauri/src/workspace.rs` - cancellable phase state machine, retry-target representation, and race tests.
- `src-tauri/src/rpc.rs` - non-blocking switch orchestration, transition event, cancel RPC, and binding tests.
- `src/rpc/bindings.ts` - regenerated TauRPC contract only.
- `src/App.tsx` - transition subscription/generation guard, snapshot preservation, and IssueExplorer remount key.
- `src/components/WorkspaceSelector.tsx` - pending Cancel and categorized Retry/Dismiss feedback.
- `src/App.test.tsx` - application-level switching/race/reset tests.
- `src/components/WorkspaceSelector.test.tsx` - selector control and error-presentation tests.
- `e2e/issue-list/fixtures/workspace.ts` - distinguishable/overlapping-ID fixtures.
- `e2e/issue-list/scripts/run-scenario.ts` - atomic scenario fixture and temporary command-delay setup.
- `wdio.issue-list.conf.ts` - select the atomic scenario/spec if a separate spec is added.
- `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.success.spec.ts` or `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.atomic.spec.ts` - real-desktop atomic switching assertions.
- `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md` - update the removed async-switch gap.

## New Files

- `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.atomic.spec.ts` - optional focused real-desktop atomic/pending/cancel scenario, if retaining the existing success spec’s narrower issue-list purpose is clearer.

## Dependencies

- Task 2 must resolve the retry-target public contract before Tasks 3–5 can implement Retry consistently.
- Task 1 must expose safe phase operations before Task 3 can release the runtime mutex during commands.
- Task 3’s transition payload/RPC contract must land before Tasks 4 and 5; generated bindings follow Task 3.
- Task 4 supplies handlers/state semantics consumed by Task 5.
- Tasks 6 and 7 follow their respective backend/frontend behavior; Task 8 depends on the final transition transport and UI selectors.
- Task 9 follows the settled e2e implementation and requires a shell-enabled `bw` environment to post the issue comment.

## Risks

- **Underspecified Retry identity (blocking):** The current state clears `pendingWorkspace` for every failure, yet the requirements require Retry to replay the valid failed target. The product/API must explicitly choose an ephemeral `retryWorkspace` field or typed error `candidatePath` (recommended) before coding; holding a failed target in `pendingWorkspace` violates the supplied terminology.
- **Asynchronous transport design:** The current `switch_workspace` resolver holds its mutex across synchronous commands, so merely adding a frontend button cannot make Cancel or concurrent selection work. A backend transition event (or an explicitly equivalent push mechanism) is needed to expose Pending without prohibited polling; its payload/order semantics need implementation-level verification.
- **Event ordering:** Generation drops A-after-B events, but Pending and final success share a generation. Preserve per-request event ordering or add a transient event sequence if the chosen event transport cannot guarantee ordering.
- **Error categorization:** `storeReadFailed` is catalog-memory recovery, not a switch-finalization banner. `gitRootFailed` should remain inline with validation. Confirm whether a `storeSaveFailed` unrelated to switching should retain the existing Reset-local-memory panel.
- **E2E controllability:** A real pending window requires delaying `bw` without adding production test hooks. A temporary scenario-owned command wrapper is viable on the supported macOS/Unix suite but needs a Windows-compatible fallback or documented platform restriction.
- **No background refresh:** Do not add polling or periodic issue reloads. Transition notifications and explicit user actions must be the only updates.
- **Planning-environment limitation:** No shell/`bw` tool was exposed, so this planner could not run required `bw prime` or post the requested Beadwork comment. The implementer/parent must perform both in a normal repository shell.

```acceptance-report
    }

  ],

  ],

  ],

  "reviewFindings": [
    "blocker: post-validation failure has no public retry-candidate identity once pendingWorkspace is cleared.",

  ],

```

title: Switch Workspaces atomically and recover from failures
